issue_title: Add preset style selection and preview
issue_body:
Build the next creator-facing step after episode setup: let the user choose from a small set of distinct podcast visual presets, adjust basic layout and pacing options, and see an immediate preview of how their imported speakers would appear before applying the style. This should keep simple users out of a blank canvas while making the product feel like a visual podcast workspace rather than only an upload form.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
Running `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes. In the app, after the completed episode setup flow, a creator can view at least three clearly different visual style presets, change layout and pacing choices, see the preview update using the assigned Host/Guest speaker buckets, select one preset, and continue with that style visibly applied or summarized for the episode.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The first setup flow already exists, so the highest-value next move is the preset-driven visual experience promised by the vision. It establishes the default path to a polished result before investing in advanced canvas editing, audio cleanup, or export workflows._
